messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "colo-denominator-geography-misallocation",
 "title": "Colo-only apportionment plants the flag in Germany/NL while 35–40% of PDU demand sits in non-colo load with a different country mix",
 "narrative": "Every geo share divides colo MW by colo MW, but rack-PDU demand tracks TOTAL installed IT load — and the non-colo remainder is not distributed like colocation. Germany's installed load (BMWK >2,700MW) exceeds its colo figure by ~1,000MW, Switzerland is self-operation-heavy (installed-basis share 0.13 vs 0.08), and Poland's better-corroborated installed-load figure yields 0.10 vs 0.07. EUDCA confirms the gap is continental: colo+hyperscale is only about two-thirds of Europe's IT power. A venture that builds its Year-1 named-account plan on the colo-derived map will staff Frankfurt and Amsterdam while a third of the actual PDU spend — enterprise and self-operated halls in CH, PL, AT — never appears on its target list.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "geo.DE",
  "geo.NL",
  "geo.CH",
  "geo.PL"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "EUDCA: commercial colo+hyperscale provides just over two-thirds of Europe's IT power — so roughly a third of PDU-consuming load is outside the colo scope every geo.* ratio is built on",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c11",
    "ledger:geo.DE"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The colo-vs-installed divergence is country-specific, not uniform: DE's installed-load method gives 0.42 vs colo 0.50, CH's self-operated stock pushes it to ~0.13 vs 0.08, and PL's triangulated 660MW installed load implies ~0.10 vs 0.07 — the €300M gets reallocated across countries, not just rescaled",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.DE",
    "ledger:geo.CH",
    "ledger:geo.PL"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The Year-1 plan sells through large operators (colo-centric by construction), so the misallocated map inflates the reachable share of DE/NL and hides demand in CH/PL enterprise halls; the funnel's geography mix shifts against the venture's channel build",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Who is the primary buyer you'll sell through first?",
    "ledger:geo.NL",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c14"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "Each geo node already discloses its colo-vs-installed crossCheck divergence, so the issue looks acknowledged — but it is acknowledged as per-country BAND WIDTH, not as a correlated, same-direction reallocation across all countries at once. Bands on independent nodes cannot express 'the whole dimension is measured in the wrong denominator'.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A single-scope, single-source table of total installed IT load for all CE-7 countries (e.g., EUDCA country profiles) whose per-country shares match the current colo-based shares within ±3 percentage points.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.45,
  "rationale": "The scope gap is documented in the ledger's own crossChecks for four of seven countries and corroborated continentally by EUDCA; the uncertainty is whether non-colo PDU spend per MW is comparable to colo (enterprise refresh cycles are slower, partially offsetting the reallocation)",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.DE",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.42,
   "note": "Installed-load method share; mix shift, deliberately not renormalized"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.NL",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.24,
   "note": "NL colo-heavy market loses relative weight under installed-load scope"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.CH",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.13,
   "note": "Self-operated Swiss stock enters the denominator"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.PL",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.1,
   "note": "Mordor/R&M-corroborated 660MW installed load"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Publication of country-level installed IT power (not colo-only) for CE-7",
   "where": "EUDCA 'State of European Data Centres' country profiles; BMWK/Bitkom German landscape updates",
   "threshold": "Any CE country's installed-load share diverging >5pp from its colo share",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Origin of inbound PDU demand by facility type in early pipeline",
   "where": "Venture's own CRM plus PMR Poland commercial-DC series and CBRE Suisse commercial-vs-self-operated splits",
   "threshold": ">25% of qualified CE demand coming from enterprise/self-operated sites the colo map does not list",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Reconcile all seven countries onto one installed-load scope before locking Year-1 territory and account assignments",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "geo.DE",
   "note": "The DE node carries the largest band swing (−€24M TAM at the low edge) and is the cheapest to resolve — two public sources already bracket it"
  },
  {
   "action": "Add an enterprise/self-operated sales motion (TÜV-certified retrofit bundle) for CH and PL alongside the large-operator channel",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Hedges the plan against the denominator being wrong without waiting for the data"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "definition-scopedown",
 "riskId": "risk.definition-scopedown.colo-denominator-geography-misallocation"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

BMWK/Bitkom German landscape updates","threshold":"Any CE country's installed-load share diverging >5pp from its colo share","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Origin of inbound PDU demand by facility type in early pipeline","where":"Venture's own CRM plus PMR Poland commercial-DC series and CBRE Suisse commercial-vs-self-operated splits","threshold":">25% of qualified CE demand coming from enterprise/self-operated sites the colo map does not list","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Reconcile all seven countries onto one installed-load scope before locking Year-1 territory and account assignments","type":"information","voiNodeId":"geo.DE","note":"The DE node carries the largest band swing (−€24M TAM at the low edge) and is the cheapest to resolve — two public sources already bracket it"},{"action":"Add an enterprise/self-operated sales motion (TÜV-certified retrofit bundle) for CH and PL alongside the large-operator channel","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Hedges the plan against the denominator being wrong without waiting for the data"}]}
